Claude at Acme Corp

Your AI thinking partner for writing, research, analysis, and building

Choose your track

Your work matters. Claude makes it go further.

Every role at Acme Corp exists for one reason - building tools that help teams ship faster. Claude gives you leverage to do that better. Write a client proposal in minutes. Research a competitor without digging through dozens of tabs. Draft a board update that's tight on the first pass.

Claude has three escalating layers. Chat handles thinking - drafting, research, analysis. Cowork handles doing - persistent projects, file operations, connected tools. Code handles building - actual software, pipelines, integrations.

Every role at Acme Corp has access to Chat. It covers 80% of what you'll need. Cowork and Code add capability but come with additional risk. Leadership has access to all three tiers, and certain roles will too. If you think Cowork or Code would be valuable for your work, talk to your manager to request access.

Meet your thinking partner

Why we chose Claude

Anthropic builds AI with safety as the foundation - not an afterthought. That matters when your product handles customer data.

  • Enterprise-grade security - Claude is built for organizations handling sensitive data. Your conversations are private and protected.
  • Your data stays yours - encrypted in transit and at rest. Never used to train models. Conversations are not shared across organizations.
  • SOC 2 Type II certified - Anthropic is independently audited to verify its security controls work as designed, continuously, over time. Not a one-time checkbox.
  • Constitutional AI - Claude is trained to be helpful, harmless, and honest. It will push back on bad ideas and flag when it's uncertain.

Data Privacy - Read This First

Know what's safe to share

Chat - Safe for General Work

Conversations, projects, and artifacts in Claude Chat are covered by your organization's agreement with Anthropic. Use it for drafting, research, analysis, and general business work.

Sensitive Data - Use Caution

Do not share customer PII, internal financial data, or proprietary information with tools not covered by your data processing agreement. When in doubt, anonymize the data first.

Read your AI Governance Policy

Three Escalating Layers

Thinking, doing, building. Each layer adds capability.

💬
Chat
Handles thinking. Drafting, research, brainstorming, analysis. Text in, text out.
Everyone
Unlocked
📁
Cowork
Handles doing. Persistent projects, file operations, connected tools and data.
Analysts, marketing, ops
Request Access
💻
Code
Handles building. Actual software, pipelines, integrations. Requires a technical operator.
Engineering, technical roles
Request Access

Pick Your Model

Every conversation lets you choose. When in doubt, start with Opus.

Opus

Best for depth
  • Complex analysis and long documents
  • Nuanced writing with specific voice
  • Multi-step reasoning and strategy
  • Research synthesis

Sonnet

Best for speed
  • Quick drafts and brainstorming
  • Simple questions and lookups
  • Formatting and editing
  • Everyday tasks

Resources

Training

  • Anthropic Learning Center
  • Claude Documentation
  • Help Center
  • Intro to Claude Cowork (course)
  • Claude Code in Action (course)
  • How Anthropic Uses Skills (X)

Internal

  • #ai Slack channel - questions, tips, wins
  • AI Governance Policy (link to your internal doc)
  • Training recordings in Google Drive (People Ops)

Get Help

  • Ask in your AI channel for team-specific questions
  • Use Claude itself - ask "how do I use Projects?"
  • Engineering team for Code track setup issues

We build tools that help teams ship. Claude helps us move faster, think sharper, and deliver what matters.

What is Chat?

Chat handles thinking and drafting. Single-session, text-in/text-out. This is where most of your team lives today.

You talk to it like a colleague. Ask it to draft an email, brainstorm campaign ideas, analyze data you paste in, or research a topic across the web. It learns your voice and context, so the more you use it, the sharper it gets.

Set it up in three steps below.

Key Chat Features

These tools make Claude more than a chatbot.

Projects

Persistent workspaces with custom instructions and knowledge files. Create one per major initiative. Everything you upload stays available across conversations.

Deep Research

Claude searches the web, reads dozens of sources, and synthesizes a report. Use for competitor analysis, regulatory research, or any question that needs real data.

Artifacts

Interactive outputs - documents, spreadsheets, diagrams, code. Use when you need something you can edit, download, or share. Skip for quick answers.

Connectors

Connect Claude to Google Calendar, ClickUp, Excel, PowerPoint, and other tools. Pulls in context without manual uploads. Review your data processing agreement before connecting tools with sensitive data. Don't connect anything with sensitive data without approval.

Tips That Apply Everywhere

These patterns make every conversation better, no matter what you're working on.

Start fresh often

Context windows have limits. When you switch topics, start a new conversation. Long threads get noisy and Claude loses focus.

Give context, not keywords

Claude is not a search engine. Include audience, format, tone, and constraints. "Draft a 3-paragraph email to our channel partners declining this proposal because pricing is 30% above budget" beats "vendor email decline."

Verify everything

Claude is confident even when wrong. Double-check numbers, dates, regulatory claims, and anything you'll share externally. Trust but verify.

Iterate, don't restart

If the first response isn't right, give feedback: "Make it shorter," "More formal," "Add the revenue numbers." Claude adjusts within the same conversation.

Use forcing functions

Generic prompts get generic answers. These techniques get better output:

  • "Give me 10 options" - Generate many, then cherry-pick. Faster than describing the perfect answer.
  • "Play devil's advocate" - Forces Claude to argue against you instead of agreeing.
  • "Score this 0-100%" - Have Claude rate sections of your draft, then fix the weak ones.
  • "What am I not thinking about?" - Surfaces blind spots you didn't know to ask about.
  • "Rewrite this in half the words" - Tighter output than asking to "make it shorter."
  • "You're a skeptical customer reading this" - Assigning a role changes the lens. Gets honest feedback.
  • "Write something like this but for Q2" - Paste an example. Showing the format beats describing it.

Give Claude Context

The more context Claude has, the better the output. These tools help you share what's on your screen, in your head, or too tedious to type.

📷 Paste screenshots

Hit Cmd+Shift+4 to screenshot anything on your screen, then paste it straight into Claude. Faster than describing a webpage, error message, or document layout.

🎤 Use voice mode

Tap the microphone in Claude and talk instead of typing. Faster for brainstorming, explaining context, and thinking out loud.

🤖 Prompt Cowboy

A website with a library of ready-made prompts for Claude. Browse by category, copy what fits, and customize from there. Saves you from staring at a blank input box. promptcowboy.ai

📋 Build starter prompts

Before ending a conversation, ask Claude to write a starter prompt that captures the key context, decisions, and next steps. Paste that into a fresh chat and you pick up right where you left off with a clean context window.

Use Cases by Role

Select your function to see tailored ideas. The universal cases work for everyone.

Writing & Drafting

Emails, Slack messages, docs, presentations, proposals. In your voice, with your context.

Research & Analysis

Competitor research, market analysis, policy review, data synthesis. Ask Claude to go deep.

Admin & Ops

Meeting agendas, travel planning, document formatting, process documentation, templates.

Select your role to see tailored use cases and prompt ideas.

Growth / Marketing

  • Write campaign briefs from a one-line idea
  • Draft SEO content with brand-compliant language
  • Analyze competitor positioning and messaging
  • Generate ad copy variations for testing
  • Build social media content calendars

Sales

  • Research prospects before calls
  • Draft personalized outreach emails
  • Prep for sales calls with account context
  • Write proposals and RFP responses
  • Summarize call notes into action items

Product

  • Write feature specs and PRDs from rough notes
  • Synthesize user research into themes
  • Draft stakeholder updates by audience
  • Competitive analysis with feature comparisons
  • Sprint planning and scope estimation

Engineering

  • Architecture docs and design reviews
  • Code review and bug triage
  • Write and improve test coverage
  • Technical documentation and runbooks
  • Automate repetitive development tasks

Product

  • Write feature specs from a one-line idea
  • Analyze user feedback for patterns and themes
  • Draft release notes and changelog entries
  • Create competitive feature comparison matrices
  • Summarize research findings into actionable insights

Operations

  • Process documentation and SOP writing
  • Training materials for new workflows
  • Vendor evaluation and comparison
  • Draft internal comms and policy updates
  • Audit prep checklists and documentation

Customer Success

  • Analyze support ticket patterns and root causes
  • Draft customer onboarding guides
  • Build QBR presentation decks
  • Create knowledge base articles from tickets
  • Summarize feature requests by segment

Finance

  • Financial modeling and scenario analysis
  • Variance analysis and narrative writing
  • Board report drafting and formatting
  • Budget preparation and forecasting
  • Audit support and documentation

People Ops

  • Write job descriptions from role requirements
  • Build structured interview guides
  • Draft policy documents and handbook updates
  • Onboarding checklists and materials
  • Performance review templates and guidance

Operations

  • Process documentation and SOP creation
  • Vendor research and comparison matrices
  • Logistics planning and optimization
  • Incident response runbooks
  • Capacity planning and resource allocation

EA / Admin

  • Draft meeting agendas and follow-up emails
  • Travel planning and itinerary building
  • Document formatting and template creation
  • Email drafting in leadership's voice
  • Research and summarize topics for meetings

CEO / Leadership

  • Board prep - decks, memos, and talking points
  • Investor communications and updates
  • Strategic analysis and decision frameworks
  • Company-wide announcements and messaging
  • Market research and industry trend synthesis

Your Profile
2
Try It Out

Company Project

Your Profile

Tell Claude who you are and how you work.

Tell Claude who you are and how you work. We'll generate a personal profile you can paste into Claude's settings.

Who You Are

How Claude Should Work

Select all that apply
Customers / prospects
Internal team
Partners / integrations
Finance / procurement
Vendors / partners
Leadership / board
Pick the one that fits best. You can always override in a conversation.
Direct - skip the fluff
Warm - friendly but clear
Casual - like a teammate
Formal - professional tone
Select all that apply
Get to the point
Give me a recommendation
Accuracy over speed
Keep it practical
Be honest, not nice
Ownership + next steps
Think big picture
Select all that apply
Pad responses with filler
Over-explain obvious things
Use corporate jargon
Add caveats I didn't ask for
Use emojis
Hedge or qualify everything
Repeat what I just said
Be overly enthusiastic

Your Writing Voice

Paste 4-7 examples of your real writing. When you ask Claude to write a Slack post, email, or message for you, it will reference these samples to match your voice.

⚠ No sensitive data. Remove any customer names, financial details, or other identifiable information before pasting. Change names to generic placeholders if needed.
  • A wins post or team shoutout (Slack)
  • Feedback to a direct report or teammate
  • An email to a vendor or partner
  • An email to a customer or prospect
  • A summary or status update
  • A quick approval, decline, or decision
  • Copy you've written (marketing, product, internal)
1

Copy this prompt into a chat with ChatGPT or Gemini

This tells your current AI to export everything it knows about your professional context. Work context only

Export all of my stored memories and any context you've learned about me from past conversations that is relevant to my professional life. Exclude anything purely personal - relationships, health, hobbies, personal finances, etc. Focus on what would help a new AI tool understand how I work. Preserve my words verbatim where possible, especially for instructions and preferences. ## Categories (output in this order): 1. **Instructions**: Rules I've explicitly asked you to follow - tone, format, style, "always do X", "never do Y", and corrections to your behavior. Only include rules from stored memories, not from conversations. 2. **Career**: Current and past roles, companies, and general skill areas. 3. **Projects**: Work projects I meaningfully built or committed to. ONE entry per project. Include what it does, current status, and any key decisions. 4. **Preferences**: Working-style preferences, communication style, and opinions that apply broadly in a professional context. ## Format: Use section headers for each category. Within each category, list one entry per line, sorted by oldest date first. Format each line as: [YYYY-MM-DD] - Entry content here. If no date is known, use [unknown] instead. ## Output: - Wrap the entire export in a single code block for easy copying. - After the code block, state whether this is the complete set or if more remain.
2

Paste the results below

Copy ChatGPT or Gemini's response and paste it here. This will be combined with your answers into one profile block for Claude.

Your Preferences
Copy this into Claude Settings > Profile > Preferences. This is what Claude reads every conversation.

Review this and make it yours. Click the text above to edit - add context about your team, current projects, or how you prefer to work.

Enable Capabilities

While you're in Settings, go to Capabilities and turn on all three:

  • Memory
  • Search across chats
  • Code execution

Under Tool access, select Load tools when needed.

Profile created. Claude now knows your voice and how you work.

Try It Out

Open Claude and try something real.

Your preferences are already loaded. Start a conversation and see your voice come through.

I just finished setting up Claude at work. Help me write a short Slack message for our #ai channel. Ask me a few questions first - what am I most excited to use it for, and one way I think it'll help my work. Then draft the message.

Claude will ask you a few questions, then draft the message in your voice. That's your preferences working.

Once you're happy with it, paste it in #ai. If the tone feels off, tell Claude: "Make it more direct/casual/concise." It adjusts.

First conversation done. You're already ahead of most users.

Company Project

Give Claude deep context about your company.

In the Claude app, click Projects in the left sidebar. Click + New Project. Name it Acme Corp. Under visibility, select Private.

Download these two files. Open your Acme Corp project, find Project knowledge below the project name, and click Add content to upload both.

Download company.md Download brand.md
You're set up. Claude has your voice, your context, and your project. Scroll down for features, tips, and use cases for your role.
Chat setup complete.You gave Claude context most people skip. That makes all the difference.

Try something new

Paste one of these into Claude Chat and see what comes back.

Draft a response to a customer asking why our enterprise plan costs more than competitors.

What questions should I ask a channel partner about their onboarding timeline and support expectations?

Write a one-paragraph explanation of what Acme Corp does for a prospect who's never heard of us.

Help me outline a presentation on why investing in project management tools improves team velocity.

What are the most common objections prospects have during a demo, and how should we address them?

Draft talking points for a call with an enterprise partner about integration timelines and SLA expectations.

Explain the difference between our Standard and Pro tiers to someone evaluating both options.

Write a short FAQ for customers about what's included in each pricing tier.

What does a strong business case for adopting new software include? Give me a checklist I can share with buyers.

Help me write a Slack message to the team announcing a new product feature. Keep it short and energizing.

Draft a follow-up email to a prospect who attended a demo but hasn't moved forward yet.

What are the key metrics a SaaS company our size should track weekly? Help me build a scorecard.

I need to explain our onboarding process to a new hire. Write it as a simple step-by-step guide.

Compare the pros and cons of selling direct vs. through channel partners for a B2B SaaS product.

Write a 60-second elevator pitch for Acme Corp that I could use at a tech conference.

What is Cowork?

Cowork is the desktop app's project mode. You give Claude access to a folder on your computer and it works independently - reading and editing files, connecting to Google Calendar and Drive, browsing the web, and producing real deliverables like spreadsheets, reports, and dashboards.

Best for: Operations, marketing, finance, and anyone who works with documents, data, or repetitive knowledge work. No technical background needed.

Cowork: Claude Works on Your Files

What You Can Do With It

Calendar Audit

Claude pulls 3 months of Google Calendar data, categorizes your meetings, and shows where you're overcommitted. Takes about 2 minutes.

Data Dashboard

Drop an Excel file and ask for a dashboard. Claude builds an interactive HTML report with charts you can share with your team.

Marketing Report

Connect to your analytics and Claude builds a performance summary. Open rates, pipeline numbers, campaign ROI - formatted and ready to present.

Downloads Cleanup

Point Claude at your Downloads folder and it organizes files by type, finds duplicates, and flags anything older than 90 days for cleanup.

Ready to try it?

Cowork reads and edits files on your computer, connects to tools like Google Calendar and Drive, and runs tasks independently. That power comes with some risk, so access requires manager approval.

Chat with your manager about getting access. If you've already completed the Chat track, mention that - it helps.

Cowork Features

What makes Cowork different from Chat.

Plugins + Slash Commands

21 official plugins add capabilities. Type / to access built-in workflows like /explore-data, /build-dashboard, and /performance-report. Enable or build custom plugins under Customize in settings.

File Access

Cowork reads and writes files on your computer. Drop a folder into a project and Claude can analyze, edit, and create files inside it.

Connectors

Connect to Google Calendar, ClickUp, and other tools without downloading files. Be careful with connected accounts that may have access to sensitive data. Review your data processing agreement for details.

Multi-Step Workflows

Give Claude a complex task and it executes multiple steps autonomously. Research, analyze, generate reports - all in one conversation.

Scheduled Tasks

Set up recurring tasks that run on a schedule - daily briefings, weekly roundups, inbox digests. Claude runs them automatically while your machine is on. Learn how to set them up.

Data Privacy: Do not share customer PII, internal financial data, or proprietary information in Cowork prompts or project files without reviewing your data processing agreement.

Cowork Tips

Structure your project folder

Don't dump everything in one folder. Use subfolders that match how you think about the work:

  • references/ - Read-only context (brand guides, policies, data)
  • outputs/ - Files Claude creates for you
  • done/ - Completed work you want to keep

Claude reads the folder structure and uses it to decide where to save new files.

Add a CLAUDE.md file

Create a file called CLAUDE.md in the root of your project folder. Write instructions that apply to every conversation: your role, what this project is, formatting preferences, things to avoid. Claude reads it automatically at the start of every session. Think of it as persistent instructions you don't have to repeat.

Combine plugins

The real power is stacking plugins. Use Google Calendar + Data to analyze meeting patterns. Use Slack + Marketing to audit your channel messaging. Multiple plugins activate automatically in the same conversation.

Delegate with three questions

Before handing off a task, answer these: What does "done" look like? What context can't Claude guess? What constraints apply? The clearer you are upfront, the less back-and-forth you need.

Ask for a changelog

When Claude edits or creates multiple files, ask it to write a what-changed.md documenting every decision it made. Review that before accepting the work. Especially important for anything you'll share externally.

Back up critical files

Before letting Claude reorganize, rename, or edit important files, make a copy. Cowork can move fast and undo isn't always straightforward. A quick duplicate of the original folder takes 10 seconds and saves you from a bad day.

Test on dummy files first

If you're asking Claude to process, reorganize, or transform a batch of files, run it on a small test set first. Verify it interprets your instructions correctly before pointing it at the real data.

Keep the desktop app open

Closing Claude Desktop kills any running Cowork task. If you've kicked off a long process (research, file reorganization, scheduled task), leave the app running until it finishes.

Cowork vs Chat

Use Chat for thinking, writing, and conversation. Use Cowork when you need file access, plugins, or multi-step workflows that touch your tools.

Research Preview

Cowork is in Research Preview (beta). Expect rough edges. Save important outputs to files. Don't rely on it for time-sensitive deliverables yet.

Get Started

Set up a project folder, then try real tasks below.

Here's what your project folder will look like when you're done:

📁 Claude_Company
📄 company.md - business context, strategy, team
📄 brand.md - colors, fonts, design tokens
📄 writing-style.md - your personal writing voice

Cowork reads files from a folder on your personal computer. You'll create a dedicated folder and fill it with context files that teach Claude about your company.

  1. Create a folder called Claude_Company inside your user folder. On Mac that's /Users/yourname/Claude_Company. On Windows it's C:\Users\yourname\Claude_Company.
  2. Download these two files into that folder:
Download company.md Download brand.md
  1. Open the Claude desktop app and click the Cowork tab in the sidebar
  2. Click Set project folder and select your Claude_Company folder

Create your writing style guide

In the Chat wizard you gave Claude writing samples and preferences. Now turn that into a reusable reference file. Open a Chat conversation and paste this prompt:

Based on my writing samples and preferences, write a concise writing style guide that captures my voice. Cover: tone, sentence structure, how I open and close messages, signature phrases, and things I never do. Format it as clean markdown I can save as a file called writing-style.md.

Copy the result, then switch to Cowork and paste this prompt:

Create a file called writing-style.md in my project folder with this content: [paste your writing style guide from Chat here]

Cowork reads this file in every conversation. Whenever you ask it to write something, it matches your voice automatically.

  1. Verify: ask Cowork "What files do you see in this project?" - it should list all three files

These files give Claude context about your company in every Cowork conversation. It uses your brand colors, knows your business, and writes in your voice without you explaining it each time.

Calendar Audit

Open Cowork and paste the prompt below. Make sure Google Calendar is connected. In the Claude app, click your profile icon, then Settings, then Plugins. Look for Google Calendar in the list and enable it. If it's not available in your organization, skip to the Downloads task below.

Pull my calendar for the past 3 months. Categorize every meeting by type (1:1, team sync, external, focus time, other). Show me: total hours in meetings vs. available time, which meeting types eat the most time, and any meetings that look redundant or could be async. Put the results in a clean summary.

A breakdown of where your time actually goes. Most people are surprised by the results.

Clean Up Downloads

In Cowork, click Set project folder and select your Downloads folder (or add it as an additional folder).

Before you start: If your Downloads folder contains files with customer PII, financial data, or other sensitive information, skip this task or move those files out first. Review your data processing agreement for details.
Scan my Downloads folder. Organize files into subfolders by type (documents, images, installers, spreadsheets, other). List anything older than 90 days that's safe to delete. Don't move or delete anything yet - just show me the plan and let me approve.

A sorted view of your Downloads folder with a cleanup plan you approve before anything moves.

Both tasks use your personal data (calendar, files). Nothing leaves your machine. All processing happens locally through the Claude desktop app.

You'll install three plugins, analyze a sample dataset, and produce a dashboard + deck. This is the kind of work that takes hours manually. Cowork does it in minutes.

If a slash command isn't recognized, the plugin didn't install correctly. Go back to Step 1 below and verify all three plugins appear in your plugin list under Customize.

Step 1: Install plugins

In Cowork, click Customize > Browse plugins. Install these three:

  • Data - Analysis, dashboards, and data validation
  • Marketing - Campaign performance, channel analysis, content insights
  • Customer Support - Ticket analysis, CSAT trends, escalation patterns

After installing each one, you'll see it appear in your plugin list under Customize. All three should show as installed before continuing.

Step 2: Download the sample data

Download sample-data.xlsx

Three tabs of 12-month fake data: Sales, Marketing, Support. Save it to your Claude_Company folder.

Step 3: Explore the data

/explore-data sample-data.xlsx

Claude profiles each tab - shape, quality, patterns, outliers.

Step 4: Marketing deep-dive

/performance-report Analyze the Marketing tab from sample-data.xlsx. Break down performance by channel, identify the highest-ROI channels, and flag any spend that isn't converting.

Step 5: Support deep-dive

Analyze the Support tab from sample-data.xlsx. Show ticket volume trends, average resolution time by category, CSAT trajectory, and flag any escalation spikes.

Step 6: Build the unified dashboard

/build-dashboard Build an interactive HTML dashboard from sample-data.xlsx covering all three departments. Include the marketing channel analysis and support ticket insights from the previous steps. Monthly trends, key metrics with month-over-month changes, cross-department executive summary. Use brand colors from brand.md.

Step 7: Validate

/validate

Claude checks the dashboard for calculation errors, methodology issues, and potential data quality problems. Review what it flags and fix anything that looks off.

Step 8: Build the deck

Create a 6-slide PowerPoint executive summary. Slide 1: title + top 3 takeaways. Slides 2-4: one department per slide with top 3 metrics and trends. Slide 5: cross-department insights. Slide 6: recommended actions. Brand colors from brand.md. Save as quarterly-review.pptx.

What you just did: Installed role-specific plugins, used slash commands for domain analysis, built a branded interactive dashboard, validated it, and created a presentation-ready deck. All from one spreadsheet.

You're all set with Cowork!

Try it on your own projects. Start with small tasks and build from there.

Available Plugins

20 plugins with 126 skills. Click a plugin to see its skills. Hover or tap a skill for details.

Anthropic plugins are free and open source. Install from the Claude desktop app under Customize > Browse plugins. Source: github.com/anthropics/knowledge-work-plugins

What is Claude Code?

Claude Code is an AI pair programmer that lives in your terminal. It reads your codebase, writes code, runs tests, and ships pull requests. You describe what you want built and it handles the implementation - editing files, running commands, and managing git workflows directly on your machine.

Best for: Engineering and technical team members who write or maintain code. You don't need to be a developer, but you do need to be curious.

Want 1-on-1 help getting started? Ask in your team's AI channel and someone will walk you through it.

Code: Claude as Your Pair Programmer

What the Team Has Built

Focus Tab

Chrome extension for tracking focused work time. Built and deployed entirely with Claude Code. View it live

This Onboarding Page

The page you're reading right now was built and is maintained using Claude Code. Every feature, test, and deploy goes through it.

Internal Tools

Scoreboards, brand guides, FAQ bots - the tools on Dream Team are built with Claude Code handling everything from architecture to deployment.

Ready to try it?

Claude Code writes real code, creates branches, and opens pull requests in production repos. It's powerful, and mistakes push to real systems, so access requires manager approval.

Chat with your manager about getting access. Prior experience with the Chat and Cowork tracks helps your case.

Code Features

What makes Claude Code a full development partner.

Plan Mode

Start complex tasks with "plan this" and Claude maps out the approach before writing code. Review and approve the plan, then execute step by step.

CLAUDE.md

Your project's instruction manual for Claude. Define conventions, architecture, and rules. Every conversation in the project follows them automatically.

/save Command

Save your conversation state at any point. Pick up exactly where you left off in a new session. Critical for multi-day projects.

Context Management

Clear context, start fresh threads, and manage what Claude remembers. Short, focused sessions produce better code than marathon conversations.

Git Integration

Claude creates branches, writes commits, opens PRs, and manages your git workflow. Tell it what to build and it handles version control.

Data Privacy: Do not share customer PII, internal financial data, or proprietary information in prompts or project files. Claude Code has full read/write/execute access to your machine - only grant it access to directories you intend to work in.

Code Tips

Start with Plan Mode

For any task with more than one step, start in Plan Mode. Claude's plans are better than jumping straight to code. Review before executing.

Keep sessions short

Context windows have limits. Start a new Claude Code session for each distinct task. Resume with /save if you need continuity across sessions.

Be specific in prompts

Name the exact file, function, and behavior you want. "Fix the login bug" is worse than "In auth.ts, the JWT expiry check on line 45 should use >= not >".

Review before accepting

Claude writes good code but makes mistakes. Review every change before committing. Use git diff to see exactly what changed.

Get Started

Start with the setup card below, then work through the exercises.

Claude Code lives inside the same Claude app you downloaded in Step 1. It reads, writes, and executes code on your machine. Different from Chat or Cowork - this is for building software, editing files, and shipping code.

1. Open Code mode

In the Claude desktop app, click the Code tab in the sidebar.

2. Install Git

Paste this into Claude Code. If Git is already installed, it'll skip ahead.

Run `git --version` to check if Git is installed. If it's not found, run `xcode-select --install` and wait for it to finish. Tell me the result.

3. Log into GitHub

You need access to your team's GitHub org. Paste this into Claude Code:

Run `gh auth status` to check if I'm logged into GitHub. If not, run `gh auth login` and walk me through it. Use HTTPS and a browser-based login. Tell me the result.

If the clone in the next step fails with a permissions error, ask your manager to add you to the team's GitHub org.

4. Clone the team skills repo

Run these commands: mkdir -p ~/team_claude git clone https://github.com/your-org/claude-skills.git ~/team_claude/claude-skills If the directory already exists, run `cd ~/team_claude/claude-skills && git pull` instead. Tell me the result.

5. Run the installer

Run `~/team_claude/claude-skills/setup.sh --all` and tell me what it installed.

This installs team skills (/ship, /save, /plan, /review, and more), sets your model to Opus, and copies context files to your Claude config.

6. Verify

Type /help in Claude Code. You should see team skills listed. If you see them, you're set.

Going further: You can also run Claude Code in VS Code (install the "Claude Code" extension from the marketplace) or directly in your terminal (npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code). Same commands, same skills, different interface.

A pricing calculator helps prospects estimate costs for your product. You'll build a working version from a single prompt. Claude Code creates the files, writes the code, and produces something you can open in your browser.

  1. Create a folder on your Desktop called pricing-calculator. In Claude Code, paste:
cd ~/Desktop && mkdir -p pricing-calculator && cd pricing-calculator
  1. Paste this prompt:
Build a pricing calculator for Acme Corp plans. Save it as index.html in this directory. Inputs: - Plan tier: dropdown (Starter, Professional, Enterprise, Custom) - Team size: number input (1-500) - Billing cycle: toggle (Monthly, Annual) Output: estimated monthly cost, annual savings if switching to annual billing, and a recommended next step (like "Start a free trial" or "Contact sales for a custom quote"). Design: - brand colors: navy #1e293b, gold #f59e0b, teal #10b981, cream #f8fafc - Inter for headings and body text - Responsive, clean, production-quality Use realistic but FAKE placeholder pricing. These are not real numbers. Add a visible disclaimer: "Estimates only. Actual pricing may vary based on your specific needs and contract terms. Contact sales for exact pricing." Include a "Start Over" button to reset the form.
  1. Watch Claude build the estimator. It creates the file, writes all the code, and tells you when it's done. This takes 30-60 seconds. You'll see Claude show what files it's creating as it works.
  2. Open the result in your browser. Find the index.html file in your pricing-calculator folder and double-click it, or ask Claude Code: "Open index.html in my browser."

What you practiced: Building a complete tool from a single detailed prompt. The key was being specific about inputs, outputs, design, and constraints. The more detail you give Claude Code, the closer the first attempt gets to what you want.

Take the estimator you just built and ship it to your configured domain. That's the section at the bottom of the homepage where team projects land. Claude Code handles the git workflow - you tell it what to do in plain English, and it creates branches, commits code, and opens pull requests on your behalf.

Before you start - clone the project repo

You need the project repo on your machine. Paste this into Claude Code:

Run: git clone https://github.com/your-org/your-project.git ~/Desktop/your-project && cd ~/Desktop/your-project If the directory already exists, run: cd ~/Desktop/your-project && git pull Tell me the result.
  1. Paste this prompt:
Ship the pricing calculator I built to the team site. Steps: 1. Create a feature branch called feat/pricing-calculator 2. Copy ~/Desktop/pricing-calculator/index.html to a new pricing-calculator/ folder in this repo 3. Add to permissions.json - restrict to my email only for now 4. Add my project to the SANDBOX_APPS array in index.html (the homepage, not the pricing-calculator one). Follow the format of the commented-out example. 5. Commit with a clear message 6. Push and open a PR Use /ship when ready.

/ship pushes your code, runs tests, and creates the pull request in one step.

  1. Claude Code runs each step and shows you what it's doing. When it finishes, it gives you a link to the pull request on GitHub.
  2. Share the PR link in #engineering for review. A teammate approves it. (For this exercise, you can approve it yourself on GitHub.)
  3. Once the PR is merged, the site auto-deploys in 1-2 minutes. Your project shows up in the Sandbox section at your configured domain.

What you practiced: The full deployment workflow - feature branch, permissions config, PR creation, review, and auto-deploy.

You're set up with Claude Code!

Pick a real project and start building. Plan Mode + specific prompts = best results.

Ways to Use Claude Code

Three entry points, same capabilities. Start with the desktop app and move to terminal or VS Code as you get comfortable.

Desktop App (Easiest)

Open the Claude desktop app and click the Code tab. Same interface you already know from Chat and Cowork. No terminal required. Start here.

Terminal

Run claude from any directory in your terminal. Faster for experienced developers who live in the command line. Full keyboard control.

VS Code Extension

Install the Claude Code extension in VS Code. Claude runs in a sidebar panel alongside your editor. Best for developers who want AI help without leaving their IDE.

Claude Team Setup
